statement.p: The concentration of a drug in a patients blood stream, in milligrams per liter, can be modeled by the function Cm>, with C(t)={\frac{3t}{t^{2}+6}}\text{,} where t is the number of hours since the drug is injected. Use technology to complete the following sentence.
The drugs concentration is 0.4 milligrams per liter hours after injection and then again hours after injection.

solution.p: To locate the points on C(t) where the y-value is 0.4\text{,} we use technology to graph the function C(t)={\frac{3t}{t^{2}+6}} and y=0.4\text{,} and then locate their intersections.

The intersections are (0.91,0.4) and (6.59,0.4)\text{.} Those points imply the drug concentration in the blood stream will be approximately 0.4 milligrams per liter approximately 0.91 hours since injection, and then again at this level 6.59 hours since the injection.
